name: Austin YIMBY Action
tagline: More homes, more neighbors, more Austin.
mission: We advocate for abundant housing at every price point in Austin through land use reform, transit-oriented development, and streamlined permitting. We believe the housing crisis is a supply problem with a supply solution.

political_positioning.description: Pro-development, pro-density, pro-transit. Fiscally pragmatic. Socially liberal. Not affiliated with any party but aligned with pro-housing Democrats and moderate Republicans on land use issues.

key_policy_positions: Streamline permitting to reduce housing construction timelines
Advocate for the Jimmy Clay/Roy Kizer golf course redevelopment
Oppose organized NIMBY efforts to block infill development

active_campaigns.title: Support 445-Acre Golf Course Rezoning
active_campaigns.summary: The city has initiated rezoning of the Jimmy Clay and Roy Kizer golf courses. This is the single largest housing development opportunity in Austin. We need organized pro-housing voices at every public hearing.
next_actions: Monitor for public hearing schedule announcement
Prepare template public comment supporting residential/mixed-use zoning
Coordinate with AURA on joint advocacy strategy

active_campaigns.title: District 1 Council Race — Pro-Housing Candidate Engagement
active_campaigns.summary: Open seat in District 1 (East Austin). This council member will vote on every housing issue for 4 years. We need to engage candidates on housing positions early, before the field solidifies.
next_actions: Draft housing policy questionnaire for D1 candidates
Identify and recruit potential pro-housing candidate if current field is insufficient

active_campaigns.title: Pct 4 Commissioner Runoff — Voter Mobilization
active_campaigns.summary: May 26 Democratic runoff. County commissioners influence land use and transportation infrastructure. Low turnout = high leverage for organized groups.
next_actions: Research both candidates' positions on development
Organize precinct-level voter outreach (target: 5-10 volunteers, 4 weekends)

aligned_organizations.name: AURA
aligned_organizations.relationship: Strong ally — coordinate on most housing issues

engagement_pipeline.awareness: Social media, local press coverage of housing issues
engagement_pipeline.interest: Attend one of our monthly briefings or read our issue guides
engagement_pipeline.action: Show up at a public hearing, submit public comment, contact your council member
engagement_pipeline.leadership: Join the steering committee, lead a campaign, represent us at planning commission

immediate.action: Set up candidate questionnaire for D1 race

medium_term.action: Prepare testimony toolkit for golf course rezoning hearings

strategic.action: Build coalition with AURA for November council elections
strategic.notes: Combined endorsement slate across Districts 1, 3, 5, 8, 9 would have significant influence
